danger_signals.visual_form: Darkened/obscured map areas outside unit sight range
danger_signals.meaning: Unknown territory; potential enemy presence; hidden threats

danger_signals.visual_form: Red-colored enemy units on minimap and main view

danger_signals.meaning: Objective locations; often contain hostile forces or dangerous encounters
Base under assault; resource production threatened

danger_signals.visual_form: Mountains, cliffs, rivers, seas rendered distinctly
danger_signals.meaning: Impassable or difficult terrain; strategic chokepoints

safety_signals.visual_form: Green-colored units; allied heroes with portrait display
Cluster of faction-colored buildings in starting area
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; resource generation; unit production hub

safety_signals.visual_form: Faction-specific architecture (Human towers, Orc barracks, etc.)
safety_signals.meaning: Controlled territory; defensive positions; support available

safety_signals.visual_form: Clear, well-lit map areas with no fog of war
safety_signals.meaning: Known safe space; no active enemy activity in view

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Humans appear hostile in red coloration when enemy-controlled
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Medieval European-inspired architecture; castles, towers, farms; formal settlements

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Orcs rendered in opposing player color when hostile
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tribal/shamanic structures; crude fortifications; organic building designs

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Night Elf units adopt enemy player color scheme
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Forest-integrated architecture; tree-based structures; mystical glowing elements; moon/night aesthetic

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Undead retain grotesque appearance regardless of alignment
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Necromantic architecture; crypts, plague structures; decay and corruption visual motifs; graveyard aesthetics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Demonic unit presence; corrupted terrain; dark auras around affected areas; supernatural visual effects
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy aggression patterns; multiple unit attacks; coordinated hostile movements

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Green toxic coloration spreading; undead unit clusters; corrupted landscape aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Undead unit spawning; plague mechanics; terrain alteration over time

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Limited visible gold/lumber deposits; sparse resource icons on map
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Worker units actively gathering; slow unit production; base expansion actions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Colored settlement clusters; faction-specific building designs dominating landscape; minimap color distribution
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Defensive structure placement; patrol unit movement patterns; base consolidation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Top bar time display; lighting changes affecting map visibility; environmental mood shift
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unit visibility changes; some units gaining/losing advantages based on time of day

environmental_storytelling.example: Human structures display formal masonry and geometric design; Orc structures show crude log construction and organic layouts; Night Elf buildings integrate with trees; Undead structures feature decay and necromantic symbols
Campaign maps show ruins of previous conflicts; corrupted lands indicate Burning Legion/Scourge presence; controlled settlements show prosperity vs. abandoned areas showing conflict aftermath
Named heroes (like Thrall) displayed prominently in top-left portrait; larger, distinctly-designed units communicate campaign importance
Objective markers positioned at strategic locations communicating mission goals and directing player attention to narrative-significant areas

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Icons appear in bottom-left corner; unit stops animation; can be reassigned to tasks
Movement toward resource nodes; looping gather animations; resource depletion visualization
Attack animations; health bar reduction visualization; death animations faction-specific
Portrait display in top-left; larger visual scale; special ability effect visibility; glowing auras indicating power level
Partially rendered/scaffolding visualization; progress bar display; resource drain indicator
Special unit emergence animations; visual aura indicating active production; queue indicator

notes: Warcraft III uses a sophisticated visual communication system leveraging faction color-coding, architectural differentiation, and dynamic UI elements to communicate world state. The fog of war mechanic is central to danger signaling—unexplored/unmonitored areas represent unknown threats. Faction identity is primarily conveyed through color schemes (blue/red/purple/green), unit silhouettes, and environmental design reflecting cultural archetypes. The top-down isometric perspective with slight rotation limits visual communication depth but is compensated through UI clarity and minimap integration. Campaign narrative is communicated through hero unit prominence, quest markers, and environmental storytelling via terrain state and building destruction/construction.
